en_body:
Hoàng-Ân...........................

...........................................
......................... 365 NIGHTS
...........................................

"...let the devil take tomorrow...Lord, tonight i need a friend...Help me make it through the night..."
The number 365 is not so long...and in Love, dreaming Time drifts...the restless day awaits returning night to turn and toss in longing remembrance. Space aches with adoration, floating silently through every atomic grain of air: as though longing to join the separated distance. The immense seas bind vision to the horizon, the day's star Sun and the moon-year satellite caress the night with constellations glittering teardrops upon the ultimately forbearing atmosphere, expanding the realm of the universe through the concept of Infinity; high mountains extend their peaks to pierce clouds and mist serenely joining at summits covered in snow and ice, standing patiently for wind and water to turn them worn; ancient forests imprison beasts of prey together with birds and reptiles and insects: mysteries challenging the distant traveler; valleys of small streams with wildflowers and fields embracing hidden-enduring villages: all conceal the human form of the beloved!
365 times night returns as the Earth completes another whole cycle around the fiery solar center. The entire universe is silently Present according to the laws of Nature...only we, human beings whose minds keep seeking from the desire to transcend the limits of the Mind. 365 of the dark nights carry violent storms within the secrets of the bottomless realm of Consciousness: the invisible world of feeling-thought (emotion and thought) becomes more real than the visible world outside. Circumstance does not define the qualities of Being, but the human struggle with the complex and manifold instincts belonging to the spiritual world creates Circumstance itself. 365 nights: more than enough exhaustion of feeling-thought, yet too brief for dreaming Love!
365 nights: aching eyes searching for one hand to hold one whole hand! 365 nights: lips faded from their ancient color await the kiss await difficulties smoothing straight the creases of hardship! 365 nights: burning teardrops kindle the smoldering fire of Love!
(đêm nghé ngọ remembering Mother Bích-Nhung)

note: The opening quotation is from "Help Me Make It Through the Night," while the closing private phrase addresses Mother Bích-Nhung.
